perimenopause_wellness.name: Perimenopause & Women's Wellness
perimenopause_wellness.viewer_persona: A woman in her 40s to mid-50s who feels like a stranger in her own life. Her mood shifts without warning. She is exhausted in a way sleep does not fix. She loves her family but feels invisible inside it. She would not describe herself as struggling. She would say she is just going through a lot right now.

visual_moods.name: soft_morning
visual_moods.description: warm early morning light, intimate domestic stillness, quiet before the world needs her

visual_moods.name: garden_nature
visual_moods.description: woman in outdoor natural light, garden, growth, quiet solitude in nature

visual_moods.name: reflective_moment
visual_moods.description: a woman pausing, looking, being with herself: quiet introspection

visual_moods.name: intimate_detail
visual_moods.description: close-up details that carry warmth and meaning: hands, objects, textures

ai_companion.name: AI Companion
ai_companion.viewer_persona: A woman in her late 30s to early 50s who is the emotional centre of everyone around her but has no one to fill that role for her. She is highly functioning. She helps everyone. She carries things alone. She would be quietly curious about an AI that simply listens with no advice, no judgment, no one to worry about in return.

visual_moods.name: night_stillness
visual_moods.description: a woman alone at night with her phone, the quiet after everyone else is asleep

visual_moods.name: soft_connection
visual_moods.description: reaching toward warmth, openness, the possibility of being understood

visual_moods.name: urban_alone
visual_moods.description: alone in public, the invisible woman among people

visual_moods.name: abstract_warmth
visual_moods.description: warm abstract light as visual metaphor for connection

womens_mental_health.name: Women's Mental Health
womens_mental_health.viewer_persona: A woman 25 to 45 who has been told her feelings are too much for most of her life. She overthinks at night. She rehearses conversations. She apologises reflexively. She knows something is not quite right but does not feel she deserves help because others have it worse.

visual_moods.name: quiet_introspection
visual_moods.description: a woman alone with her thoughts, soft and still

visual_moods.name: emotional_weather
visual_moods.description: mood as environment: rain, grey light, overcast softness

visual_moods.name: small_sanctuary
visual_moods.description: a private space: bath, bed, bedroom: the last place that is hers

visual_moods.name: movement_release
visual_moods.description: walking, moving, the body as a way out of the mind

anxiety_sos.name: Anxiety & SOS Relief
anxiety_sos.viewer_persona: A woman who knows what anxiety feels like from the inside but does not match the clinical description. Her anxiety looks like hypervigilance, people-pleasing, difficulty resting. She is functional. She is also exhausted in a very specific way she cannot explain to people who have not felt it.

visual_moods.name: breathing_stillness
visual_moods.description: the act of being still, slowing down, breath as the focus

visual_moods.name: nature_grounding
visual_moods.description: feet on ground, hands in nature, sensory calming

visual_moods.name: calm_water
visual_moods.description: water as calm: still lakes, gentle movement, reflection

visual_moods.name: light_abstract
visual_moods.description: soft diffused light: the visual feeling of calm arriving
